qa_pairs.question: What is Gaussian, and on which national compute systems is this computational chemistry application currently supported?
qa_pairs.answer: Gaussian is a computational chemistry application produced by Gaussian, Inc. It is currently supported on the Nibi and Fir national compute clusters.

qa_pairs.question: What type of parallel execution is supported for Gaussian jobs on the national compute systems, and what is the main consequence of this limitation?
qa_pairs.answer: Only "shared-memory multiprocessor parallel execution" is supported for Gaussian jobs on national systems; "Cluster/network parallel execution," also known as "Linda parallelism," is not supported. The main consequence is that no Gaussian job can use more than a single compute node.

qa_pairs.question: What are the key conditions a user must agree to in order to use the Gaussian software on Alliance national systems?
qa_pairs.answer: To use Gaussian, users must agree that they are not members of a research group developing competitive software, will not copy or distribute the software, will properly acknowledge Gaussian Inc. and the Alliance in publications, and will notify the Alliance of any changes to this acknowledgement. If a user is sponsored, their Principal Investigator (PI) must also have such a statement on file.
